content.full_text:
July 27, 1976

76-07-171

Douglas Barrett
Administrative Assistant
California Labor Federation, AFL-CIO
995 Market Street, Suite 310
San Francisco, CA 94103

Dear Mr. Barrett:

     This letter will confirm your telephone conversa-
tion with Bob Stern in which he indicated that the staff has
concluded that Mr. Henning, a registered lobbyist, may not
serve as an honorary co-sponsor of a fundraising dinner for
a state candidate. The staff believes that serving as a co-
sponsor of a fundraising dinner necessarily would constitute
arranging for the making of a contribution within the meaning of Government Code Section 86202.

     This issue, however, will be presented to the
Commission during its August meeting in connection with the
hearing on the opinion requested by Janet Adams, No. 75-173.
If you wish to participate in that hearing, please contact
Carla Bailey at (916) 322-5901 so that a convenient time for
your testimony can be arranged. If the Commission deems
that the informal staff advice contained herein is incor-
rect, I will contact you following the August meeting.

Very truly yours,

Kenneth Finney
Chief
Legal Division

KF:blh

CALIFORNIA LABOR FEDERATION, AFL-CIO

JOHN F. HENNING
Executive
Secretary-Treasurer

ALBIN J. GRUHN
President

GENERAL

Lloyd J. Lea

VICE PRESIDENTS
At Large
James P. McLoughlin
Harold Filmore
Edward T. Shedlock
James J. Trombley
Hal Shean
John F. Crowley
Steve Edney
Fred D. Fletcher
Frank S. McKee

995 MARKET STREET, SUITE 310
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94103
415 986-3585

July 14, 1976

Mr. Ken Finney, Chief, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Finney:

By telephone Friday morning, I discussed the following
question with Mr. Mark Merin of your staff. He
suggested it be submitted to you for written response.

Supporters of the campaign of a member of the
legislature have requested Mr. John Henning,
Executive Secretary-Treasurer of this Federation
and a registered lobbyist, to serve as an honorary
co-sponsor of a fund raising dinner for the candidate
prior to the General Election.

It was represented that he could serve as an honorary
dinner sponsor provided he did not purchase any
tickets nor solicit others to do so. We were told
this had been "cleared" with the FPPC.

My inquiry is to secure clarification of this point.
I will appreciate an early response in order that
a timely and appropriate response to the request
cited can be made.

Sincerely yours,

Douglas Barrett
Administrative Assistant to the
Executive Secretary-Treasurer
